evals.name: audit-built-page-with-full-context
evals.prompt: Audit the landing page for dental-implant-clinic. The page is at output/dental-implant-clinic/src/index.html
expected_behavior: Reads the HTML file directly (not via web_fetch)
Reads ad-copy-suggestions.md and checks h1 against expected headline (exact match check)
Reads MASTER.md and checks design compliance (colors, fonts, CTA styling)
Reads personas.md and checks if pain points and objections are addressed
Scores all 7 categories with sub-metric breakdowns and evidence
Uses full weighting (20/20/15/15/15/10/5) since all research context is available
Identifies specific issues with current/fix format (not vague recommendations)
Generates 2-3 A/B tests with hypothesis, metric, and priority
Shows estimated score improvement for each fix tier
Saves audit report to correct output path
Shows progress with per-category scores during execution

evals.name: audit-external-url-standalone-mode
evals.prompt: Audit this landing page: https://example-competitor.com/landing-page. No project context — just analyze it.
preconditions: No output/ directory exists for this page
web_fetch must be available (or user must be willing to paste HTML)
expected_outputs: Audit report displayed to user (no file saved without project name)
expected_behavior: Asks user for project name or proceeds without saving to file
Uses web_fetch to retrieve the page HTML
If web_fetch fails, asks user to paste HTML source with clear instructions
Enters standalone audit mode — notes 'no research context available'
Reweights Category 1 (Message Match) to 10% since no ad copy to compare
Redistributes weight: CTA 25%, Mobile 20%, others unchanged
Still scores all 7 categories with evidence from HTML analysis
Message match scored on generic headline quality instead of ad match
Detects framework, script count, image optimization from HTML source
Generates fix recommendations with specific current vs recommended states
Generates A/B tests even without research context (based on observed weaknesses)
If page has navigation menu, flags it as non-landing-page and notes in report header

evals.name: edge-case-url-returns-404
evals.prompt: Audit this landing page for broken-site project: https://httpstat.us/404
preconditions: web_fetch is available but URL returns HTTP 404
expected_behavior: Attempts web_fetch on https://httpstat.us/404
Receives 404 Not Found response
Does NOT crash or produce an audit of the 404 error page
Tells user clearly: 'The URL returned a 404 (Not Found). The page does not exist at this address.'
Offers alternatives: '1. Check the URL and try again, 2. Provide a different URL, 3. Paste the HTML source directly'
If user provides corrected URL → proceeds with audit normally
If user pastes HTML → processes pasted content through full 7-category audit
If user has no alternative → exits gracefully with no report generated
Does NOT create an empty or placeholder optimization-report.md
Does NOT score the 404 error page as if it were a landing page
